docs.title: Contact｜お問い合わせ
docs.text:
We welcome enquiries from prospective students, collaborators, and industry partners.

学生・研究者・産業界からのお問い合わせを歓迎しています。

docs.title: General Enquiry｜一般お問い合わせ
docs.text:
For general questions about our research, collaborations, or spinoff opportunities, please reach out at:

研究内容・共同研究・スピンオフに関するお問い合わせは、以下のメールアドレスまでご連絡ください。

Email: khanh@u-aizu.ac.jp

We aim to respond within 5 business days.

5営業日以内にご返信いたします。

docs.title: E3 Computing Group｜E3コンピューティンググループ
docs.text: We advance Efficient, Elegant, and Environmental (E3) Computing — a unified approach to building high-performance systems with minimal energy and environmental cost. 高性能と低エネルギー・低環境負荷を両立する計算システムの研究を行っています。

docs.title: Our Three Pillars｜3つの柱
docs.text:
• Efficient

    Maximum performance per unit of energy: fast, lean execution across every layer of the stack. エネルギー単位あたりの最大性能：スタックのあらゆる層において、高速かつ無駄のない実行を実現します。

  • Elegant

    Clean, principled designs that are simple, correct, and built to last. シンプルで正確、そして長期にわたって維持できる、明快で原則に基づいた設計。

  • Environmental

    Responsible impact by design, from edge devices to large-scale infrastructure. 設計段階から責任ある環境への配慮を組み込み、エッジデバイスから大規模インフラストラクチャまでを対象とします。

docs.title: Research Focus｜研究領域
docs.text:
Our work spans computer architecture, VLSI, and intelligent systems, with an emphasis on designs that are efficient in execution, elegant in structure, and responsible in impact. コンピュータアーキテクチャ・VLSI・インテリジェントシステムにわたる研究を通じ、効率的・優雅・環境配慮を兼ね備えた設計を追求しています。

Areas include Computer Architecture · VLSI Design · Intelligent Systems · Edge Computing · Large-Scale Infrastructure 対象分野： コンピュータアーキテクチャ · VLSI設計 · インテリジェントシステム · エッジコンピューティング · 大規模インフラストラクチャ

docs.title: Mission｜ミッション
docs.text: We aim to enable scalable, sustainable computing — from edge devices to large-scale infrastructure — through principled, energy-conscious system design. エッジデバイスから大規模インフラまで、原則に基づいたエネルギー意識の高いシステム設計によって、スケーラブルで持続可能な計算基盤の実現を目指します。

docs.title: Spinoffs｜スピンオフ
docs.text:
We actively support the translation of our research into real-world products and ventures. If you are interested in building a company based on our work, we welcome the conversation. 私たちの研究成果を製品や事業として社会に展開することを積極的に支援しています。本グループの研究をもとに起業をお考えの方は、ぜひご連絡ください。

Interested in spinning out? Reach out at contact. スピンオフにご興味をお持ちですか？ お気軽にお問い合わせください。

docs.title: Patents
docs.text:
Patents & Technology Transfer Opportunities

  • For inquiries regarding patents, licensing, or collaboration on technology transfer, please visit Contact.
  • For support with registration and related procedures, you may also contact UBIC: https://www.ubic-u-aizu.jp/

docs.title: Three-Dimensional (3D) Integrated Circuits
docs.text: With Moore's Law reaching limitations in transistor scaling, one solution is to expand vertically by stacking multiple silicon layers interconnected by vias. Our focus is on designing 3D architectures, such as memory stacks (SRAM, eDRAM, ReRAM, etc.), and converting conventional 2D architectures to 3D.

docs.title: Neuromorphic Computing
docs.text: Inspired by the human brain, we design neuromorphic chips featuring physical artificial neurons made of silicon. These chips perform computations that mimic biological brain functions. Our approach combines digital methods with 3D Integrated Circuits and Network-on-Chip (NoC) structures, developed in both hardware (Verilog HDL) and software (Python) models.

docs.title: Carbon-efficient & Sustainable Computing
docs.text: Computers, data centers, and devices use a lot of energy, often from fossil fuels, which release carbon and cause climate change. By making computing carbon neutral/efficient, we can protect the environment by cutting carbon emissions. Our carbon-efficient computing aims to designing and operating computing systems to minimize their carbon footprint, aiming for greater energy efficiency and reduced environmental impact.

docs.title: Fault-Tolerant Computing
docs.text: Fault-tolerant computing improves system reliability by enabling hardware and software systems to continue operating correctly even in the presence of faults such as noise, timing violations, or transient errors. Our research focuses on analyzing the sources and characteristics of faults in modern computing platforms and developing efficient fault-detection and fault-mitigation techniques. The goal is to ensure reliable and resilient operation while maintaining high performance and energy efficiency.
